questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To provide a marketplace for buying and selling Notion templates
options.explanation: The marketplace is mentioned as a lesson, not the main purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To help users learn Notion, earn badges, and become certified admins
options.explanation: The article states that Notion Academy teaches Notion, offers badges, and provides a certification path.

options.text: To offer live technical support for Notion bugs
options.explanation: Support is provided through the Help Center, not the Academy.
Notion does not sell hardware; this option is unrelated.

questions.question: What do you receive after completing a Notion learning path?

options.text: A discount on Notion subscription plans
options.explanation: Discounts are not mentioned as a reward for completing paths.
The article explicitly says you can earn badges by completing paths.
Templates are taught in lessons, but they are not given as a reward.

options.text: Automatic admin rights on any workspace
options.explanation: Admin rights are unrelated to completing a learning path.

questions.question: Which of the following lessons is NOT listed in the Featured Lessons section?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is listed as a beginner lesson.
This lesson appears in the featured list.
Project management is one of the featured lessons.
Advanced SQL is not mentioned anywhere in the article.

questions.question: To become a Notion Certified Admin, which of the following steps is implied by the article?

options.text: Complete the Notion Certified Admin learning path
options.explanation: The article encourages users to get certified by completing the relevant path.
There is no mention of an additional fee for certification.

options.text: Obtain a recommendation from a current Notion admin
options.explanation: Recommendations are not part of the described process.

options.text: Pass a written exam administered by Notion support
options.explanation: The article does not reference an exam; certification is tied to the learning path.

questions.question: Which path is described as the "Advanced Path" in the article?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is for beginners.
Workflows Path is labeled intermediate.
The article lists an "Advanced Path" with five lessons.
There is no "Beginner Path" label; the beginner offering is the Essentials Path.

questions.question: Which lesson is categorized as "Intermediate" and focuses on "Templates & marketplace"?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is a beginner lesson.
The article lists this lesson as intermediate.
Project management is also intermediate but focuses on tasks, not templates.
Workspace design is labeled advanced.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between learning paths, badges, and certification in Notion Academy?

options.text: Completing a learning path automatically grants Notion certification.
options.explanation: Certification is a separate program; completing a path earns a badge, not a certification.

options.text: Learning paths lead to badges; certification requires additional criteria beyond the paths.
options.explanation: The article explains you earn badges by finishing paths, while certification is a distinct, higher‑level achievement.

options.text: Badges are only awarded after you become a Notion Certified Admin.
options.explanation: Badges are earned directly from paths, independent of admin certification.

options.text: Certification replaces the need for badges and learning paths.
options.explanation: Certification complements, not replaces, the badge system.

questions.question: If a user wants to focus on building automation and AI‑powered systems, which learning path should they prioritize?

options.explanation: Essentials Path covers basic skills, not advanced automation.
The Workflows Path is described as creating connected systems with AI.
While Advanced Path includes automation, the article specifically ties AI‑enabled workflows to the Workflows Path.
A single lesson is helpful but the full Workflows Path provides broader coverage of automation and AI.
